title: 2026 Oscars Predictions: The Most Known Unknown Race
text: IndieWire's Awards Editor Marcus Jones examines the surprisingly unpredictable 2026 Academy Awards race in his newsletter The Lead Up. Despite One Battle After Another being the dominant Best Picture frontrunner by nearly every metric, the competition maintains genuine suspense across numerous categories. Frontrunners: Jones identifies several near-certain winners. One Battle After Another is expected to sweep Best Picture, Best Director, Best Adapted Screenplay, Best Cinematography, and Best Editing. Sinners appears positioned to win Best Original Screenplay, Best Original Score, and Best Casting—marking significant career milestones for director Ryan Coogler, composer Ludwig Göransson, and casting director Francine Maisler. Competitive Races: In acting categories, Michael B. Jordan (Sinners) leads Best Actor after winning at the Actor Awards, though margins remain razor-thin. Wunmi Mosaku (Sinners) is favored for Best Supporting Actress following her BAFTA victory. Sean Penn (One Battle After Another) leads Best Supporting Actor. Jessie Buckley (Hamnet) is positioned as the frontrunner for Best Actress. International and Specialty Categories: Sentimental Value (Norwegian) appears favored for Best International Feature following its BAFTA win, while Guillermo del Toro's Frankenstein dominates technical categories including production design and costume design. Netflix shows strength in animated and short categories with KPop Demon Hunters and documentary selections. Jones emphasizes: the fun for me this year has been that anything can happen. He celebrates the unpredictability, noting the Oscars ceremony on March 15, 2026 offers genuine uncertainty despite clear frontrunners.
